channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A nonprofit leader, a social worker: Here are the stories of the people on Biden's clemency list
item.description: Among the roughly 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted or who were pardoned by President Joe Biden are a nonprofit leader who supports at-risk youth, a social worker who fosters animals and a counselor and recovery coach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Olympic and Paralympic officials put coach on leave after AP reports sexual abuse allegations
item.description: The U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Committee has placed an employee on administrative leave after The Associated Press reported that one of its coaches was accused of sexually abusing a young biathlete, causing her so much distress that she attempted suicide.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and is pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas sues DuPont, 3M over 'forever chemicals' in consumer products
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton (R) is suing companies that have historically made toxic “forever chemicals,” commonly referred to as PFAS, or products that contain them, alleging false advertising over their safety.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, ABC 4

item.title: Back trouble and brain fog bothered suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing, his posts show
item.description: After Luigi Mangione made the difficult decision to undergo spinal surgery last year for chronic back pain, he became a proponent of the procedure that changed his life for the better.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department, Louisville reach deal after probe prompted by Breonna Taylor killing
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The U.S. Justice Department and the city of Louisville have reached an agreement to reform the city’s police force after an investigation prompted by the fatal police shooting of Breonna Taylor and police treatment of protesters, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Dakota regulators OK underground storage for proposed Midwest carbon dioxide pipeline
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota regulators approved permits Thursday for underground storage of carbon dioxide delivered through a massive pipeline proposed for the Midwest, marking another victory for a project that has drawn fierce opposition from landowners.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fentanyl found inside dolphins in Gulf of Mexico
item.description: Traces of fentanyl were found inside bottlenose dolphins that were found dead in the Gulf of Mexico, raising concerns that the deadly drug may be affecting other sea life.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: Asked at a Chinese Foreign Ministry briefing on Thursday about Trump's invitation, spokesperson Mao Ning responded: “I have nothing to share at present.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Black denomination upholds stance against same-sex marriage. A gay pastor in its ranks seeks change
item.description: When the African Methodist Episcopal Church, arguably the world’s largest independent Black Protestant denomination, held its quadrennial General Conference in Ohio in August, among the agenda items was an issue that the Rev. Jennifer S. Leath had labored over for two decades: same-sex marriage.
item.dc:creator: DARREN SANDS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will TikTok be banned in January? Here's where things stand as deadline nears
item.description: You may not be able to doomscroll on TikTok much longer unless the social media company finds a way to stop a new law from going into effect.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: Making a $1B investment in the US? Trump pledges expedited permits — but there are hurdles
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is promising expedited federal permits for energy projects and other construction worth more than $1 billion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mangione family in Maryland: What we know
item.description: Although many people outside of Maryland came to know the "Mangione" name only after the arrest of Luigi Mangione, 26, who is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, the family has a number of prominent ties to the state where he grew up.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, Fox 5

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 bodies recovered amid debris from a fishing boat that reportedly capsized in Alaska
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Two bodies have been recovered amid debris from a fishing boat that reportedly capsized with five people aboard in rough seas in waters off southeast Alaska earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Altoona McDonald's slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: An Altoona McDonald's is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the murder of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Still rockin': As singer turns 80, the Christmas song she sang as a teen is a holiday staple
item.description: Her memories of recording “Rockin' Around the Christmas Tree” are a little hazy. She remembers the producer placing decorations around the studio and blasting the air conditioner on a warm Nashville day to create a holiday spirit. The musicians, she recalls, nailed it in a couple of takes.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER AP Media Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Descendant of last native leader of Alaska island demands Japanese reparations for 1942 invasion
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Helena Pagano's great-grandfather was the last Alaska Native chief of a remote island in the Bering Sea, closer to Russia than North America.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden creates Native American boarding school national monument to mark era of forced assimilation
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — President Joe Biden designated a national monument at a former Native American boarding school in Pennsylvania on Monday to honor the resilience of Indigenous tribes whose children were forced to attend the school and hundreds of similar abusive institutions.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about Luigi Mangione, the suspect arrested in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, ABC 4

item.title: VSP charged two for hit-and-run crash that killed a North Carolina woman
item.description: Virginia State Police are investigating a deadly multi-vehicle accident on I-81 in Wythe County that killed a North Carolina woman and seriously injured two others on November 30.
item.dc:creator: Thad Randazzo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge dismisses charges against Karen Read supporter who scattered rubber ducks and fake $100 bills
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Massachusetts judge dismissed criminal charges Monday against a backer of Karen Read who admitted placing dozens of yellow rubber ducks and fake $100 bills around town in support of Read. Richard Schiffer Jr.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gas prices drop to lowest in 3 years, AAA says
item.description: Good news for the millions of drivers on American roads: gas prices are falling. The national average for a gallon of gas has hit a three-year low, the American Automobile Association (AAA) recently announced.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, WGN 9

item.title: Forever chemicals tainting food supply, destroying American farmers
item.description: Advocates like Erin Brockovich are sounding the alarm. She says when it comes to forever chemicals, “the storm is here.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, WGN 9

item.title: Ohio bill would require high-demand liquor bottles to be opened when sold
item.description: The bill aims to combat "flippers" who quickly buy up the limited supply of highly sought-after spirits in Ohio to illegally resell them on a secondary market for significantly more than what they paid.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, WGN 9
